observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records at least quarterly, in accordance with the aforementioned regulations and immediately record the necessary correcting entries to ensure that the balances of the Cash in Local Treasury are reconciled.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of paying of RPT shares due to barangays without the approved disbursement vouchers; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all payments are duly covered with approved disbursement vouchers pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 and Section 344 of the Republic Act No. 7160.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, pursuant to Sections 182, 187, 189 and 257 Volume 1 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to set up the proper safeguards on various supplies, drugs and medicines that: 

The GSO to accept the deliveries from suppliers and issue Requisition and Issue Slip for its transfer to the end-user. He should also maintain stock cards for each inventory items and submit to the Municipal Accountant the required Report of Materials and Supplies Issued five days after the end of the month;

The Municipal Accountant record all inventories as asset upon delivery and acceptance and maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each specific type of Inventories; and 
The Municipal Mayor require periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. The physical count of unissued inventory items by type shall be conducted at least once a year and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant is now using the Asset Method in recording and Perpetual Inventory Method starting January 2021. All purchases are recorded as asset and the Supply Officer summarizes monthly Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and submits the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis of recording expenses for the month.

The GSO shall furnish the accounting department the necessary records on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts; and 

Instruct the accounting office to make the necessary adjustment in order to recognize the unrecorded transactions pursuant to Sections 187 and 188 of the GAM for LGUs issued under COA Circular No. 2020-004 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit immediately the significant details and information embodying the reconciling items identified in the bank reconciliation. We also remind that separate bank reconciliation statement should be prepared for each bank account of the LGU to easily trace and identify the reconciling items, if any between the accounting records and bank statements.

We recommended that the Management instruct the collectors and all other local accountable officers to maintain and use cashbooks for proper monitoring of collections and remittances, thus, precluding possible mishandling and misappropriation of collections pursuant to the requirements of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Properly monitor and account for her daily collections, issue Official Receipts for all collections, in acknowledgement thereof, regardless of nature and ensure that the same be deposited intact on the next banking day to the depository banks to avoid exposure of the funds to mishandling and misuse in accordance with Section 62 (C) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and to preclude the occurrence of shortages or overages in accountability;
Immediately issue an Official Receipt for the cash overage amounting to P33,319.52 uncovered in the cash examination, pursuant to Section 40 of the same Manual.
We also recommended Management to remind the Municipal Treasurer to keep her personal monies separate and distinct from her collections, pursuant to Section 312 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the projects to be programmed under the 20% Development Fund partake the nature of investment and capital expenditure and falls within the allowed projects that would contribute to socio-economic development and environmental management of the Municipality pursuant to Section 3.0 of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of IRR of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 and instruct the LDRRM Officer to submit the required report within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate the timely review of the transactions. We further recommended that Management comply with the disclosure requirement in the said Circular.

We recommended that Management include projects/activities/programs chargeable against the “Trust Liabilities- DRRMF” account in the annual LDRRMFIP pursuant to NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013  and the same  be formulated in substantial adherence to the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management 	consider most advantageous prevailing prices to properly estimate Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) pursuant to Section 36 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9184 to avoid incurrence of excessive disbursement of government funds.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184 particularly in ensuring that the performance security is properly forfeited in case of default by the Contractor/s.

We recommended Management to:
Submit in writing an explanation for the non-compliance with the requirements of the aforesaid DWPH Department Order in the preparation of the ABC; and
Henceforth, ensure that the Municipal Engineer and the concerned personnel responsible in the preparation of the ABC to strictly comply with the rules and regulations in the preparation of cost estimates for all projects to avoid the incurrence of excessive contract costs.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that at least five percent (5%) of the total agency budget appropriations authorized under its annual GAA correspond to activities addressing well-defined gender issues;
Exert efforts to implement all program, projects and activities integrated in the GAD plan and budget to fully attain the objectives of the GAD of the Municipality; and
Results of quantifiable programs and projects should be presented in the annual accomplishment report to assess performance based on the approved plans and budgets.

We recommended that the Management fast-track the implementation of the subject priority projects in order to avoid accumulation of unimplemented projects and to ensure the optimal utilization of 20% development fund in the ensuing year.

We recommended that Management ensure that taxes are properly withheld from income of the LGU personnel pursuant to the provisions of BIR Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated July 31, 2018 amending Section 2.79 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-1998.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their action/s undertaken and proposed action plans on the audit findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI for submission to the COA Auditor within five (5) days from receipt of the AOM.
We further recommended that henceforth, the Management submit regularly to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain subsidiary ledger for cash advances drawn against the 20% Development Fund for the proper monitoring and reporting of said fund; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of daily, monthly and yearly ending balances of subsidiary ledger with the cashbook of the Disbursing Officer.

We recommended that Management submit in writing a justification for the observation and to refrain from using disbursements out of the 20% Development Fund or other funds to liquidate financial assistance received as Trust Fund from other agencies.

In view of the forgoing observations, we recommended the following:

a. Require the municipal accountant to record purchases of various inputs, such as, chicken dung,    saw dust, inoculants and other raw materials for the production of bio-organic fertilizer as raw materials inventory and recognize it as work in process inventory when they are finally issued and utilized in the production process. Recognize work in process inventory account expenses for labor, electricity, fuel, depreciation of the facilities and equipment used, and other expenses related to the production of bio-organic fertilizer. 

b.	Require the person in charge with the production process at the Materials Recovery Facility at barangay Fusina to coordinate with the municipal accountant and update her with the status and volume of bio-organic produced. Physical inventory count and reconciliation of their records is an indispensable procedure in the establishment of proper cost per unit of producing the bio-organic fertilizer which finally facilitate the quantitative evaluation and study of the results of operation. This internal control procedure allows the check and balance between custodial and recording functions. In this manner, produce process, production are properly accounted and safeguarded from possible loss.

c. 	Require concerned officials to pass an appropriate ordinance thru the Sangguniang Bayan relevant to the sales, disposal and utilization of the bio-organic fertilizer produced by the municipality.
We recommended that management to avoid advance payment on government contracts and adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 88 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
We recommended management to prepare and submit its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within the period prescribed by the law.
